programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the felines of Ryann and Derek, who do not get along - Buddy has resorted to being a full time bully to the more placid Lita. Plus, everything goes smoothly between two flatmates until one gets a cat named Princess Puffy Pants, which urinates on everything

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: The family go to South Africa to continue Steves global conservation dream. Terri helps release a wild leopard, while Bindi meets an all-female anti-poaching team

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia tries to rescue a scared pit bull abandoned by its owners in an empty house, then has more to worry about when her own dog, Jethro, falls dangerously ill

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates Louises cat Pip, a one-eyed cerebral hypoplasia feline, which will not stop tormenting and attacking the owners other cat, Red. Plus, Erins moggy Autumn causes havoc by urinating in several places around the home, including the settee, beds, kitchen worktops, microwave, computer monitor and backpacks

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps expectant parents Sara and John with their aggressive cat, Lucy. Plus, Keri wants to become an animal psychiatrist, but the daily fights between her two felines make her doubt her chosen career path

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens investigate a suspected poacher, while an alligator and her hatchlings must be captured and relocated from an East Texas school

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and his team head to Mexico, where vet care is unaffordable to most, to open a mobile clinic and help as many people and pets as they can

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson returns to build more spectacular treehouses, beginning with a five-platform structure for a nature preserve in Michigan

programme.title: 100 Days Wild
programme.desc: Olivers refusal to help prep fish is the last straw for Andrew and Jennifer. A sudden temperature plunge cuts Adam and Evans bear hunt short

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The return of the reality show following the lives of Alaskan family the Kilchers. In the first episode, the family faces the fallout from the warmest winter in decades, as Atz Lee and Elvin tackle an emergency mission to secure their cabins at the head of the bay

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Birdie goes on a treacherous hunt to bring home meat for the family and save her chickens from slaughter. Later, Noah constructs an automatic diaper machine

programme.title: Mountain Monsters

programme.desc: The hunters track down one of the mysterious cloaked figures at the heart of the Spearfinger mystery. However, the red shed might hold all the answers

programme.desc: The family go to South Africa to continue Steves global conservation dream. Terri helps release a wild leopard, while Bindi meets an all-female anti-poaching team

programme.title: Richard E Grants Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: The actor explores the evolutionary roots of lust - the intense, out-of-control craving for sexual interaction. Animals feel lust as well as humans, otherwise species would die out

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: Moving day arrives for Acco, the zoos biggest crocodile. But his mate Cassie goes into hiding as the team races to reunite the pair in their new enclosure

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Wardens Boone and Iles rescue a tangled 12-foot alligator. Meanwhile, a large number of poached birds are found on an abandoned boat

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: Part two of two. Professor Jim Al-Khalili visits the Gran Sasso National Laboratory in Italy, where scientists are attempting to capture and study dark matter. He also reveals how the discovery that the expansion of the universe is accelerating has led to theories that a mysterious new force called dark energy is in the process of gradually tearing the universe apart

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: In the early 19th century Staithes was as one of the largest east coast fishing ports, and even now the harbour building renovations reveal the seaside villages impressive financial boom. Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the harsh North Yorkshire coastline that surrounds Staithes, as he finds out how the village was built to be a safe haven. It was also a settlement rich in the natural resource of shale. Contributors include local author Peter Appleton and fellow archaeologist Caroline Barrie Smith

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes a 20-mile walk along the Kennet and Avon Canal, learning about the history of the waterway, which provided a connection between the countrys two most important ports, Bristol and London. She also examines how the canal was restored to its former glory with lottery funds, before concluding her journey at the Caen Hill flight of locks

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell narrates this Timeshift documentary telling the story of how Britains phone network was built over the course of 100 years, from early call boxes to the Post Office Tower, and its impact on the public

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Thorpeness in Suffolk, the first purpose-built seaside village in the UK. The ingenuity of amenities such as the hand-dug lake, country club and inland housing were the unique vision of Glencairn Stuart Ogilvie. Hamish Ogilvie helps Ben to understand his great-grandfathers vision, stemming from theatrical inspiration

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter travels to north Wales where, more than 200 years ago, engineer Thomas Telford created a 126ft-high aqueduct that spans the vale of Llangollen. She finds out why it has become a World Heritage Site by taking a six-mile walk along the winding Dee Valley, finishing at the landmark Telford described as a stream through the skies

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day

programme.title: Platoon (1986)
programme.desc: A raw recruit is thrown into the thick of the Vietnam War along the Cambodian border, where he finds himself caught up in a lethal feud between two sergeants. Oliver Stones Oscar-winning drama, the first of his Vietnam trilogy, starring Charlie Sheen, Willem Dafoe, Tom Berenger, Forest Whitaker, Kevin Dillon, Johnny Depp and John C McGinley

programme.title: Oliver Stone: Face to Face
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs talks to the writer and director Oliver Stone about his life and movies, which at the time of recording included Platoon, Born On the Fourth of July and Wall Street. First broadcast in September 1990

programme.title: The Bridge at Remagen (1969)
programme.desc: As Allied forces prepare for a major assault on Germany, a small unit of American soldiers is sent to capture a strategically important bridge over the Rhine - but German forces are determined to prevent the Allied advance, and seek to destroy it. Second World War adventure, starring George Segal, Robert Vaughn, Ben Gazzara and Bradford Dillman

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Thorpeness in Suffolk, the first purpose-built seaside village in the UK. The ingenuity of amenities such as the hand-dug lake, country club and inland housing were the unique vision of Glencairn Stuart Ogilvie. Hamish Ogilvie helps Ben to understand his great-grandfathers vision, stemming from theatrical inspiration

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter travels to north Wales where, more than 200 years ago, engineer Thomas Telford created a 126ft-high aqueduct that spans the vale of Llangollen. She finds out why it has become a World Heritage Site by taking a six-mile walk along the winding Dee Valley, finishing at the landmark Telford described as a stream through the skies

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1999 and featuring Texas, Sugar Ray, Travis, Shania Twain, Sixpence None the Richer, Hepburn and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 June 1999 and featuring Sixpence None the Richer, The Wiseguys, Garbage, Chicane feat Maire Brennan, Supergrass, Shed Seven, Jamiroquai and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the chart show programme, first broadcast on 11 March 1982. Includes performances from Bow Wow Wow, Fun Boy Three and Bananarama, Depeche Mode, Julio Iglesias, Haircut 100, The Associates, Adrian Gurvitz and Tight Fit

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo, Sybil Ruscoe and Rod McKenzie host the edition first shown on March 16, featuring performances by New Order, Gloria Estefan, Chanelle, Fuzzbox, Madonna, Soul II Soul with Caron Wheeler, Jason Donovan and Guns N Roses

programme.title: St Patricks Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of classic pop performances from the BBC archives to celebrate St Patricks Day, reflecting the huge impact that music from Ireland and Northern Ireland has made across the globe. Featuring U2, Sinead OConnor, Van Morrison, the Undertones, Thin Lizzy, the Pogues, the Corrs, the Cranberries, Boyzone and Westlife

programme.title: Other Voices 2026: Dingle, Ireland
programme.desc: Annie Macmanus, Huw Stephens and MayKay present music from St James Church in Dingle, County Kerry, featuring performances by Dermot Kennedy, Amble, Travy, Florence Road, Jessy Blakemore, Dry Cleaning, Shame, Dove Ellis, Miles Kane, Wunderhorse and Jacob Alon

programme.title: Enya at the BBC
programme.desc: The Irish singer and composer as seen on the BBC, including her breakthrough single Orinoco Flow, which stayed at the top of the UK charts for three weeks in 1988. Since then she has released nine top 10 albums and won multiple awards all while maintaining her privacy, rarely giving interviews and never touring

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Jo Whiley introduces a 2016 performance by the singer from the BBC Radio Theatre in front of an intimate audience, featuring songs from his album Keep Me Singing

programme.title: Boomtown Rats: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: DJ Pete Drummond introduces a classic concert by Bob Geldofs band from the Goldiggers, then a popular nightclub and concert venue in Chippenham, Wiltshire. The programme was first shown in February 1984, the same year that the lead singer would go on to found the charity Band Aid, raising money to combat poverty in Ethiopia

programme.title: The Irish Rock Story: A Tale of Two Cities
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how rock music evolved in Ireland over 40 years. The film demonstrates how the sounds that emerged were grounded in the musical traditions of the islands two main cities, from the pioneering touring showbands of the late 1950s through to the modern day. Richard Dormer narrates an examination of how the harder sounds of Belfast merged with the more folk-centric traditions of Dublin, celebrates some of the key musicians to have emerged from the Irish scene, and reveals how they were influenced by the political, social and cultural climate of their surroundings. With contributions from U2, Sinead OConnor and Bob Geldof

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1999 and featuring Texas, Sugar Ray, Travis, Shania Twain, Sixpence None the Richer, Hepburn and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr go all-out designing a Moorish-themed master bedroom. Amanda steers the ship in the bedroom whilst Alan takes charge in the adjoining lounge. However, creative tensions soon arise when Amanda wants to get rid of Alans favourite curved arch which links the spaces together. The DIY duo take some much-needed time out to explore the picturesque coastline of Nerja, but theres trouble at sea. When Alan decides to take a shortcut, he ends up capsizing their kayak

Will Kirk discovers the delicate craft of marionette making

programme.desc: An emergency in Hertfordshire uncovers a case of animal neglect and in the Scottish Highlands, fire crews battle a wildfire so large its visible from space. Paul Colgan takes part in first aid training catered specifically to farmers in Dumfries

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Paul Ainsworth, Jess Filbey and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Alex Kingston. Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Pride (2014)
programme.desc: Gay rights activists gather in London for a Pride march in 1984, and decide they have a common cause with the miners strike. When their fundraising activities are rejected by the miners union, they travel to a Welsh pit village to show their support in person. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Schnetzer, Bill Nighy, Andrew Scott and Imelda Staunton

programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Part two of two. Steve Backshall continues his adventure in the Philippines, where he swims with one of the deadliest snakes on the planet, meets a shark that spends most of its time more than 500 metres underwater, and searches in the jungle for a tarsier - a predator that eats its victims head-first

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Part one of two. Steve Backshall visits the Philippines, where he encounters creatures including an armoured predator and an arachnid with legs four times longer than its body

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mails delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: Louis immerses himself in Floridas multimillion-dollar rap scene, which is as notable for the controversy surrounding its emerging stars as its creativity. He spends time with a number of artists who are achieving success through self-promotion on social media, from those playing out fierce rivalries online to rappers live-streaming their often violent and chaotic lifestyles. While drawing in an ever-growing fanbase attracted to its unvarnished portrayal of real-life struggles and hardship, this content has also drawn the attention of the authorities, who see it as evidence of criminals indulging in illegal gang activity

programme.title: Road House (1989)
programme.desc: A philosophy-reading kung fu expert gains a reputation as the best bouncer around, and is hired by the owner of a rowdy Missouri bar to keep the peace. When he fires some of the establishments corrupt staff, he incurs the wrath of a local crime boss who has a stranglehold on the towns businesses. Action thriller, starring Patrick Swayze, Ben Gazzara, Kelly Lynch, Sam Elliott and blues singer Jeff Healey

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Its Tommys birthday, but theres no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving hes still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: Louis immerses himself in Floridas multimillion-dollar rap scene, which is as notable for the controversy surrounding its emerging stars as its creativity. He spends time with a number of artists who are achieving success through self-promotion on social media, from those playing out fierce rivalries online to rappers live-streaming their often violent and chaotic lifestyles. While drawing in an ever-growing fanbase attracted to its unvarnished portrayal of real-life struggles and hardship, this content has also drawn the attention of the authorities, who see it as evidence of criminals indulging in illegal gang activity

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mails delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Spider-Man 3 (2007)
programme.desc: A sinister alien parasite brings out the web-slinging heros darker side, filling him with thoughts of revenge on his uncles murderer, the shape-shifting Sandman. Meanwhile, he also has to contend with the challenge of his best friend following in his fathers villainous footsteps - and a jealous rival developing superpowers of his own. Superhero sequel, with Tobey Maguire, Kirsten Dunst and James Franco

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis Theroux spends time with singer-songwriter Rachel Keen at her home, backstage at Glastonbury and in a recording studio. She tells Louis about the challenges of balancing her ambitions as an artist with commercial demands and opens up about painful experiences shes suffered within the industry

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: As the rise of platforms like OnlyFans gives adult entertainment performers financial independence away from the studio model of old, Louis encounters a new generation using their online platforms to call out alleged predatory behaviour by prominent figures from the business. As a result, an industry that prides itself on pushing sexual boundaries is asking itself difficult questions about consent, and infamous figures like long-time performer Ron Jeremy are being challenged in court

programme.title: Escape Room (2019)
programme.desc: The prospect of winning a significant sum of money lures a group of individuals into a life-threatening scenario. Being locked in several rooms with extreme conditions, they discover the secrets behind the escape room and must fight to survive by finding a way out. Psychological thriller, starring Deborah Ann Woll, Taylor Russell and Tyler Labine

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions (2021)
programme.desc: The survivors of a deadly game designed by a sinister corporation are dragged back into an even deadlier trap. And as a handful of former escapees join in, the competitors must work together to outsmart their inhuman tormentors and give their all to solve increasingly sophisticated puzzles. Horror sequel, starring Taylor Russell and Logan Miller

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: As the rise of platforms like OnlyFans gives adult entertainment performers financial independence away from the studio model of old, Louis encounters a new generation using their online platforms to call out alleged predatory behaviour by prominent figures from the business. As a result, an industry that prides itself on pushing sexual boundaries is asking itself difficult questions about consent, and infamous figures like long-time performer Ron Jeremy are being challenged in court

programme.title: Living Every Second: The Kris Hallenga Story
programme.desc: Diagnosed with Stage IV breast cancer at 23, Kris Hallenga made a radical decision about what she wants to do with the rest of her life. Together with her twin sister, Maren, Kris founded the Coppafeel charity, harnessing the power of her own experience to show millions of young people how to check for signs of cancer. In 2013, Kris made a film about her life for the BBC and, over the decade that followed, continued to invite the cameras back. This film reveals how her energy, drive and ambition has left an important legacy

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs representing central England serve their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry, with Michelin-starred chef Spencer Metzger as this weeks veteran judge. Highlights include a Jerusalem artichoke and turnip dish linked to War Horse and a monochrome, art-deco styled monkfish dish that pays homage to Birminghams Electric Theatre. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: The journalist finds out what the future holds for Europe in a world where it can no longer fully rely on America, beginning by visiting Italy and Germany to explore how theyre changing in a turbulent new world. In the Tuscan hills, she joins Italian paratroopers on a Nato exercise with 1,500 soldiers from seven different countries, then she heads to Germany, where she visits a Volkswagen car factory to learn how theyre being affected by both Russia and China. And in Germanys far south, not far from Munich, she meets a family who are building a house with a nuclear bunker

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: In March 2018, a driverless car struck and killed a woman crossing the street in Tempe, Arizona, making it the worlds first pedestrian fatality from a car that was being driven by AI. But there was also a human operator behind the wheel, monitoring the vehicle, and a decision had to be made who was responsible, the human, or the AI? In her first TV interview, operator Rafaella Vasquez tells the story of the crash and its aftermath, while Hannah also talks to the lead detective on the case. She also tracks down a whistleblower from Uber, the company behind the driverless vehicle, and takes her first rides in autonomous vehicles in the US and UK

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Mum Rachel needs Bears to repair her bond with 21-year-old daughter Venice. Rachel is desperate to build a loving and respectful relationship between them - one she feels she missed out on with her own mother. As they embark on a kayaking task, it isnt long before emotions run high and tempers flare

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk Rich join local craftsmen in in Kirkcudbright, Dumfries and Galloway, to restore a memorial sculpture scarred by decades of coastal weather in time for a remembrance service for lives lost at sea. In Kincardine, Fife, Will collects Lyndseys unusual family heirloom, a cast iron pan that cooked thousands of meals, hoping electrolysis will transform it from garden gate-stop to a camping essential, before heading to the Scottish Borders to learn from some celebrated rush-seated chair-makers

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets retiree Jenny who is moving from Kent to the North Wessex Downs to be closer to her son, Ollie, and is hoping her £300,000 budget will find her a rosy cottage to call home

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don breathes new life into a tired agapanthus, pots up a fig tree for future harvests and shares a tip for growing roses. A retired GP shows Frances Tophill around his West Yorkshire garden, where prairie planting is the order of the day, Ashley Edwards explores the resilience of drought-resistant plants at RHS Hyde Hall and Arit Anderson visits the garden of two designers who have blended three distinct planting styles into one

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway takes a circular walk around Carreg Cennen Castle, one of the most dramatic, photogenic and romantic strongholds in Wales

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford takes to the skies to explore Scotlands cities, coasts and countryside. He begins by examining the origins of aerial photography itself and then takes a birds-eye view of forgotten factories, abandoned villages and secret military installations

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The popular series visits Glamis Castle, childhood home of the Queen Mother and the place where Macbeth is said to have killed Duncan. The experts value a posy ring with a macabre history, a dining table originally intended for supporting a coffin, jewellery which survived not only a fire but a torpedo attack and the fascinating scrapbook of a First World War pilot. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and David Harper assess items brought to Allerton Castle in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin finds out about Harrogates connection with Russias last tsarina

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay returns for the penultimate day of this weeks quiz as the trip heads to Kirkby Lonsdale in Cumbria. With the leaderboard tightening, presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peters Abby Cook are all vying for victory. Paul Gorton resumes his role as unreliable tour guide, scattering facts and fabrications as the competition edges closer to the finale

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Arran, Callum has to gather their flock from 4,000 acres of steep, rough hill terrain, while Zara recalls the devastating effects of his quadbike accident on that same ground. In Dornoch, Rob and Emma need the vets help when a ewe gets into trouble birthing triplets, while Ally and daughter Bethany travel by boat to gather sheep on an uninhabited island to bring them home to their croft on Lewis

programme.title: Dunblane: How Britain Banned Handguns
programme.desc: On March 13, 1996, Thomas Hamilton walked into the gym hall in Dunblane Primary School in Stirlingshire and shot dead 16 pupils and their teacher Gwen Mayor, seriously injuring 14 other pupils and two teachers. This documentary follows the campaign to ban private handgun ownership in the UK in the wake of the massacre, revealing how a group of ordinary people forced the government to ensure the tragedy could not happen again

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests OJ Borg and Karen Hauer take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the latest task, and the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Patrick OConnell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the link between a controversial AI algorithm and Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. There were allegations the company used artificial intelligence to deny insurance claims from vulnerable patients, while Mangione himself studied AI at university. Hannah travels to the US to meet a woman who lost her husband and claims it happened after he was denied healthcare by an algorithm, and she also speaks to a UnitedHealthcare insider who reveals her concerns about their use of AI

programme.title: Jack the Ripper: The Case Reopened
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Emilia Fox and Professor of Criminology David Wilson re-examine the infamous murders, which took place in the Whitechapel district of London in the late 19th century. Using state-of-the-art technology, they hope to get closer than anyone has before to answering vital questions, including who Jack the Ripper was, what motivated his crimes, how he escaped justice, and whether he could have been responsible for even more brutal murders

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin stumbles across a crime scene when he goes in search of his brother Solomon on Antigua. In a remote cabin, a local businessman has been shot. But Solomon and his associate Cheech both swear the other is responsible, and theyre not letting Mervin go until each one has convinced him of their account

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: Its Not the Worst Thing Thats Happened This Week
programme.desc: Anna is in the hospital, reeling from events of the incident and desperate for answers. In rehab, Rachel is restless and increasingly agitated, struggling through withdrawal. Meanwhile, Maggie tells Garv she wants to start IVF, but he brushes her off, reluctantly agreeing to a rigid sex schedule

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Two members of a throuple attempt to work out their issues in the wild, taking on a raft-building task and an abseil and climbing challenge that pushes their communication to the limit. In a campfire chat, they find a way to explain their fears with the help of relationship therapist Simone. Will three people prove too many for this marriage to cope with?

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, Will Kirk and Cindy Welland help restore a 19th-century grandfather clock for Doncaster School for the Deaf and in Birmingham, Dominic Chinea brings a classic coffee machine back to life for a family honouring their fathers legacy. Will Kirk discovers the delicate craft of marionette making

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive hits the road with a female Moroccan motorcycle gang and also visits the Juventus Academy to train with some of the countrys best young footballing talent. He goes antique shopping with an expert to haggle for some of Casablancas best bargains, and meets a Michelin-starred chef to grab the best morning catch to cook in his new fine-dining restaurant

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive concludes his journey by travelling to the northern tip of Morocco and meeting band the Master Musicians of Joujouka high in the Rif mountains. In a nearby farm, he grabs a bucket and helps milk goats to produce the smooth and creamy jben cheese. In the winding streets of Casablancas old town, he learns new skills from a lantern-making artisan and meets a local fashion influencer to find the perfect Moroccan kaftan.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which players compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best able to answer questions. Susan Calman hosts

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway hops across the water from Tenby to Caldey Island, which is known for its monastery, monks and lavender perfume, but is also ideal for a sunny stroll

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford explores how planners and architects took to Scotlands skies after the Second World War as they embarked on missions to redesign towns and cities. From building a motorway right through the heart of Glasgow, to one of Scotlands first tower blocks, James reveals how these utopian and controversial visions have transformed lives of people living in Scotland today

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts return to Glamis Castle, where they value a pair of Chinese Imperial bowls, a collection of more than 800 handbag mirrors, and a necklace sporting 2,000-year-old gems. Michael Aspel introduces highlights of other shows and explores the Queen Mothers childhood at this historic Scottish retreat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and David Fletcher view antiques brought in by members of the public to the Dorford Centre in Dorchester. Among the items they appraise are a pin cushion doll, a Royal Doulton Black Jack and a globe, which turns out to be the days star item. Presenter Paul Martin looks at the geology of Dorsets Chesil beach

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on double points Friday before the overall victor is revealed

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of this weeks quiz as the action arrives in Bangor. TV presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peters Abby Cook return, and Paul Gorton resumes his role of unreliable tour guide, sharing stories, surprises and suspect claims as the overall winner is revealed

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The Shannon, which runs through the heart of Ireland, is the longest river in the British Isles. For locally born naturalist and cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson, it is also the wildest. Here Colin paddles along the Shannon to reveal the creatures living in the water and along the banks, such as kingfishers, predatory pike and native red squirrels

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don kicks off the horticultural year by cutting back ornamental grasses, planting summer-fruiting raspberries and potting up dahlias. Adam Frost has moved house, and reveals his new garden. And Arit Anderson gets access behind the scenes at the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, where they do vital work in conserving endangered plants from all over the world

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Abandoned by their dominant males, the Xudum pride comes under siege from more intruder lions, and the mums with young cubs must link up with the rest of the pride for protection. Leopard mum Lediba struggles after a lion steals her kill. To feed her cub, she is forced to make a dangerous move. Pobe is teaching cheetah cubs Kea and Bo to hunt, but when a hyena pack arrives, their survival instincts are pushed to the limit. With predators all around, every mother must fight harder than ever to keep her young alive

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Host Tez Ilyas welcomes Spring Day and Ignacio Lopez to the stage at Londons Hammersmith Apollo where they each offer their unique views of life

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Girl (2023)
programme.desc: A mother and her daughter confined to their isolated world they have obsessively created to protect themselves. Drama, starring Deborah Lukumuena, Danny Sapani and LeShantey Bonsu

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: At War: Trump, Israel and Iran - Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin investigates the origins and aims of the US/Israeli offensive against Iran, and the future of the relationship between the UK and the US in light of Keir Starmers refusal to allow the use of UK bases for the first attacks. The programme asks what the campaign is achieving, how far it could escalate, and what it means for ordinary people caught in the fighting

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Veronica must decide her future now she is not currently a nun, but not fully part of the world either. The Maternity Home prepares to close its doors, but Dr Turner is determined to preserve equipment and resources for his patients until the last possible moment. The Mullucks fight for the rights of all Thalidomide victims, while Cyril and Rosalind prepare for their wedding

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers James Layte, Karen Dalmeny and Mike Melody are in Lytham St Annes, Lancashire, where they offer locals the chance to put items up for auction or accept a cash sum. A bronze bust bought by Mike proves to be one of the days highlights

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Ian Towning, Karen Dalmeny and Stewart Hofgartner hope to unearth a few treasures in Worthing, West Sussex, where one of the best deals is a gold charm bracelet

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: Leanne and Emily arrive at the scene of a car crash and race to catch a suspected dangerous driver who attempts to flee on foot. Ollie and Harry are called to investigate an altercation between housemates. In the centre of town, Petes coppers nose leads him to uncover something entirely unexpected

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: Members of the Coniston Mountain Team are summoned to help a paraglider who has crash-landed at the top of Great How Crag with potentially paralysing neck injuries. Meanwhile, a man has gone missing around the flooded tunnels of a disused quarry at Hodge Close

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Catch Me Daddy (2014)
programme.desc: A girl runs away from her strict Pakistani family so that she can be with her British boyfriend - a move that enrages her father. He sends her violent brother and a group of other men to track his daughter down. Thriller, starring Sameena Jabeen Ahmed, Gary Lewis and Conor McCarron

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth set their sights on turning the old school room into their snug. Marj restores a baroque French chair, while fellow Pottery Throw Down judge Rich Miller helps Keith to make brutalist tiles for a feature wall. The couple take a break from forging the chapels future to visit the National Library of Wales to discover Capel Salems fiery past

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help key-workers with modest budgets who want to buy their first homes in London. Civil partners Jo Hendle and Ally Macdonald have high expectations, having rented in Belsize Park, but to make the most of their £200,000 budget they need to move away from the expensive north of the capital to the more affordable south. Meanwhile, Liverpudlian Jennifer Dean hopes that £209,000 will buy her a two-bedroom period property in the south of the capital, with outside space for her cats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Isle of Wight to uncover four new items, and restorer Ronnie relives his past life on Ibiza via a three-wheeled trike

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Woody and Kellys wedding day arrives, with the pair trying to resist the temptation to consummate their marriage before they say their vows. Meanwhile, Carla predicts nothing but doom and gloom for the happy couple. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. Woody and Kellys wedding day arrives, with the pair trying to resist the temptation to consummate their marriage before they say their vows. Meanwhile, Carla predicts nothing but doom and gloom for the happy couple. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca decides to smarten up her image, but it is not long before her old smoking habit leads to a bar-room blaze. Starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day six of the Games in Italy. The alpine skiing giant slalom finals get under way at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with ParalympicsGBs Menna Fitzpatrick and her guide Katie Guest hoping to be in contention for a medal

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day six of the Games in Italy, as the alpine skiing giant slalom finals continue at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. ParalympicsGBs Menna Fitzpatrick is set to be involved and she came into these Games as Great Britains most decorated winter athlete in either the Olympics or Paralympics with six medals

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued coverage of day six of the Games in Italy, as the alpine skiing giant slalom finals conclude at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey then report on the final day of round robin matches in the mixed team wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium, where places in tomorrows medal matches are to be decided

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Debra and Gordon are looking for the perfect holiday home in the southern Spanish city of Torrevieja. This former fishing village has grown to become one of Spains most popular resorts, with acres of beautiful countryside and miles of spectacular coastlines hugging popular towns, villages and resorts. While Debra is keen on apartments, Gordon would love a townhouse for their budget of £120,000. Laura Hamilton shows them a selection of places that satisfy both of their desires, from a sea-view apartment and a property with a gym and a sauna to a tantalising townhouse

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Holiday host Donna fears that she cannot deliver the luxury stays shes promised in Italy. Theres a housing emergency at Brian and Paulas new French restaurant, and, also in France, Paul and Melissa are put through their paces restoring a once-prestigious hotel

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sue hosts the weeks final visit, at her Victorian townhouse BandB, Brooklands Guest House, in Penrith. The vintage decor is a hit with most of the guests, but things turn bittersweet after a marmalade tasting experience when discussion turns to payment day

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the penultimate night in Manchester, cabaret practitioner Vanessa is turning her modest Mancunian semi into a Chicago speakeasy, with home comfort food planned and a side order of illicit hooch. Richard is blown away by Vanessas outfit and its not long before hes accusing Danny of dressing like a Butlins red coat. Amy and Pauline have a snoop around the house and discover a treasure trove of outfits from Vanessas cabaret shows. The evening really takes off when Vanessas alter ego Coco Deville takes to the floor accompanied by a small band and some ostrich feathers. The piña colada crumble amazes Danny and Richard, and the group learn the subtle art of face yoga. Will Vanessas illicit night of burlesque bravado ruffle feathers?

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day six of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Para alpine skiing giant slalom medals were up for grabs at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, and the mixed team curling event reached its final day of round robin fixtures at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Natalie Fleet, MP for Bolsover, sets out the governments vision for tackling the cost of living crisis

programme.title: George Clarkes Building Home
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets people who are taking on challenging huge builds for powerful emotional reasons, from being drawn back to an area that theyve always loved or a place with strong family memories to taking on a house thats close to their heart. Young married couple Chris and Ellie have returned to where Chris grew up, in Lancashires Ribble Valley, to build a home together. After months of struggling to find the perfect property, they ended up buying a run-down bungalow from Chriss grandparents - but when it turned out the house wasnt suitable to live in, they began the months-long process of building a new house from scratch

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: As housemaid Emma and young master Edwin fall deeper in love, she struggles to see how they can overcome their class differences. Meanwhile, Adam and Olivia continue their own love affair as Adeles erratic behaviour and paranoia worsen. In the 1970s, 80-year-old Emma reckons with her childrens plan to oust her from the Harte empire - unaware of a secret romance blossoming between her protegee granddaughter Paula and Jim Fairley. Starring Jessica Reynolds and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-three-year-old motorbike enthusiast Scott is rushed in after crashing at high-speed at a track event, and there are concerns he may have suffered spinal injury. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Peter has potentially life-threatening injuries after being crushed under an eight-ton static caravan and 21-year-old hockey player Lizzie has severely broken her ankle during training and needs a reset

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Roofer Simon is in a critical condition after falling off a two-storey building onto concrete. Major trauma consultant Rory and the team try to assess Simons multiple major injuries and work together to try to save his life. Ninety-seven-year-old Doris is in Resus with a worryingly fast and irregular heart rate, while in the Childrens Emergency department, 20-month-old Evelyn has a burn on her finger from a mozzarella stick

programme.title: Millionaire Hoarders
programme.desc: Car fanatic John decides to part with some of his beloved collection to help fund his retirement. Expert and fellow petrol-head Clive spots two classic cars in Johns collection that could raise a huge sum, but will his gamble on an auction sale pay off? In the West Country, Rachel and Ronnie help Caroline, who inherited an inn from her father but has made the difficult decision to part with some of its weird and wonderful contents in order to invest on the building. Ronnie spots a rare animal pelt that could be the solution to Carolines problems - if he can prove its authentic

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eaterys decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brothers trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Molly vs the Machines
programme.desc: Documentary about the death of Molly Russell, the 14-year-old who in 2017 took her own life as a result of depression and negative social media content, and her father Ians fight for online safety. The film focuses on what happened to Molly in the lead-up to her tragic death and the broader economic logic behind AI and big tech as it continues to shape, influence and infiltrate peoples lives

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Isle of Wight, where they meet Helen and husband Dave to restore unwanted items including a metal garden bench and an old water feature

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, married couple Paul and Debbie kick off the competition with a menu of tried and tested dinner party favourites. However, engaged couple Angela and John arent impressed by their simple fare and posh couple Kirsty and James find Pauls behaviour even less palatable. Perfectionists Angela and John cook up dishes inspired by their favourite restaurant meals. Finally, Kirsty and James plan an organic, locally sourced menu. At the table theres a lesson in asparagus eating and the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their guests return their food criticism

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney and judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller present the final, where the three remaining potters create miniature model theatres and take on a tricky throwing technique in their last surprise second challenge. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sams regulars are enticed away to a new neon-lit bar that comes complete with free food and dancing girls. Woodys marriage seems to be in trouble, and Carla looks for another job. American bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norms boozing expertise secures him his dream job as chief beer-taster at a brewery, while Rebecca grows addicted to the bars new slot machine. George Wendt stars alongside Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sams reputation is tested by Henris challenge to decide who is the more accomplished ladies man, and the battle to collect the most telephone numbers turns into an international contest between America and France

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening events on day seven of the Games in Italy, including the start of the mens giant slalom at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, which will see ParalympicsGBs Neil Simpson go for a medal after winning bronze in this event at the 2023 Lleida World Championships

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further live coverage of day seven of the Games in Italy. Dame Sarah Storey presents from Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium, where the semi-finals of the mixed team event takes place. Elsewhere, Alice Tai reports on the para biathlon at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium, where ParalympicsGBs Scott Meenagh will hope to medal in the sprint pursuit in his third and final Paralympics

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued live coverage of day seven of the Games in Italy, including the conclusion of the para biathlon sprint pursuit at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium. Plus, 16-year-old Dom Allen from Fife makes his ParalympicsGB debut in the mens standing alpine skiing giant slalom at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: David Coulthard is joined by Ariana Bravo to present action from the battle for Sprint Race positions in round two at Shanghai International Circuit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Laura and Nick dream of making a permanent move to Spain with their beloved dog Poppy. Theyre after a property in Estepona on Spains Costa del Sol that meets both their needs and Poppys. Property expert Jean Johansson lines up five options for their £160,000 budget

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Italy, holiday host Donna tries to keep up as reservations fly in at the height of the season. In France, BandB owners Paul and Melissa have to adapt their accommodation, while Brian and Paula fear that unseasonable weather will put a dampener on their new restaurants big summer event

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, a number of BandBers are sure that their competitors have been doing some tit-for-tat marking. When the dust settles, wholl win the week?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Manchester, professional crooner Danny takes to the kitchen. As the guests travel from Spain to Thailand through the menu, Amys palette is tested with the Asian flavours. Will Dannys night be a success, or nothing to write home about? And who will win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day seven of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Neil Simpson and Scott Meenagh were looking for medals in the mens giant slalom at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre and the sprint pursuit at Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium respectively. Plus, the semi-finals of the mixed team wheelchair curling took place at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium. With reports from Sarah Storey and Alica Tai

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Helen Whately MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, sets out the Conservative Partys plan to get more people into work

programme.title: Inside the Worlds Most Luxurious Cruises
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes on board some of the worlds most luxurious cruise ships, featuring a floating art gallery boasting a multi-million-pound collection and how pets are as pampered as their owners on board the Queen Mary 2. A passenger on his first-ever clipper voyage faces his fear of heights to fulfil a childhood dream as he climbs the mast of a tall ship

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the weeks biggest and best shows

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by Richard Ayoade and Michelle Wolf for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Greg Davies: The Back of My Mums Head
programme.desc: Stand-up comedy with the Man Down star, recorded at the Lowry in Salford. Stung by his mothers suggestion that he is not normal, Greg puts everyone else under the microscope in a petty quest to discover if anyone actually is. The tour received rave reviews and was extended to 94 dates, including multiple sell-out nights at Londons Hammersmith Apollo and Southbank Centre

programme.title: Bill Baileys Remarkable Guide to the Orchestra
programme.desc: The comedian joins forces with Oscar-winning composer Anne Dudley to investigate some of the finer nuances of orchestral performance. He teams up with the BBC Concert Orchestra to reveal the lesser-known influences of some of the worlds most celebrated composers, and exhibits some of his own original works

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couples two children are struggling to save the business from their parents disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks winner is announced as the BandB owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: With Capel Salem slowly becoming a home, the next room for transformation is the Victorian-inspired parlour bathroom. Keith and Marj take inspiration from Coventry Cathedral, with Keith making a giant clay candlestick holder, while Marj sets about making her dream lampshade for the bathrooms standard lamp

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Market trader Russell and retired nurse Nicola kick-off proceedings in Essex and the evening seems to go well with one diner polishing off his own food - and everyone elses. The second night sees proud parents Sophie and Elliot host, but their guests find crab claws in their starter. On the third evening romantic duo Gemma and Glen decide to go for a more refined dining experience in the hope of winning the cash prize of £1,000. However, its Gemmas love for burlesque that grabs most attention

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie meet their neighbours after their backyards are destroyed by a hurricane. Carrie disapproves of their idea of combining the gardens when they rebuild - until the couple next door build a pool. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Cousin Danny loses his job, so Doug comes under pressure from Uncle Stu to find him a new one at IPS - a situation he is keen to avoid. Meanwhile, Arthur takes part in a test audience for a new film. Comedy, starring Kevin James, Gary Valentine and Jerry Stiller

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Incredible Lightness of Being a Baby
programme.desc: Cletus becomes a helium tycoon. Meanwhile, Marge reunites with baby Hudson

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart accidentally sees a new superhero film before its release, and realises he can use his plot-spoiling powers for his own ends. However, two movie executives will stop at nothing to keep his knowledge secret

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events of day eight of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. 2018 gold medallist Menna Fitzpatrick is expected to be among the leading contenders for this years title in the womens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, while 18-year-old Hester Poole is expected to make her debut at the Games. Elsewhere, the mens and womens snowboard banked slalom gets under way at Cortina Para Snowboard Park, with 2022 bronze medal winner Ollie Hill expected to be in action

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: Members of the Coniston Mountain Team are summoned to help a paraglider who has crash-landed at the top of Great How Crag with potentially paralysing neck injuries. Meanwhile, a man has gone missing around the flooded tunnels of a disused quarry at Hodge Close

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Catch Me Daddy (2014)
programme.desc: A girl runs away from her strict Pakistani family so that she can be with her British boyfriend - a move that enrages her father. He sends her violent brother and a group of other men to track his daughter down. Thriller, starring Sameena Jabeen Ahmed, Gary Lewis and Conor McCarron

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth set their sights on turning the old school room into their snug. Marj restores a baroque French chair, while fellow Pottery Throw Down judge Rich Miller helps Keith to make brutalist tiles for a feature wall. The couple take a break from forging the chapels future to visit the National Library of Wales to discover Capel Salems fiery past

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help key-workers with modest budgets who want to buy their first homes in London. Civil partners Jo Hendle and Ally Macdonald have high expectations, having rented in Belsize Park, but to make the most of their £200,000 budget they need to move away from the expensive north of the capital to the more affordable south. Meanwhile, Liverpudlian Jennifer Dean hopes that £209,000 will buy her a two-bedroom period property in the south of the capital, with outside space for her cats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Isle of Wight to uncover four new items, and restorer Ronnie relives his past life on Ibiza via a three-wheeled trike

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Woody and Kellys wedding day arrives, with the pair trying to resist the temptation to consummate their marriage before they say their vows. Meanwhile, Carla predicts nothing but doom and gloom for the happy couple. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. Woody and Kellys wedding day arrives, with the pair trying to resist the temptation to consummate their marriage before they say their vows. Meanwhile, Carla predicts nothing but doom and gloom for the happy couple. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca decides to smarten up her image, but it is not long before her old smoking habit leads to a bar-room blaze. Starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the opening events on day six of the Games in Italy. The alpine skiing giant slalom finals get under way at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with ParalympicsGBs Menna Fitzpatrick and her guide Katie Guest hoping to be in contention for a medal

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day six of the Games in Italy, as the alpine skiing giant slalom finals continue at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. ParalympicsGBs Menna Fitzpatrick is set to be involved and she came into these Games as Great Britains most decorated winter athlete in either the Olympics or Paralympics with six medals

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Continued coverage of day six of the Games in Italy, as the alpine skiing giant slalom finals conclude at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre. Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey then report on the final day of round robin matches in the mixed team wheelchair curling at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium, where places in tomorrows medal matches are to be decided

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Debra and Gordon are looking for the perfect holiday home in the southern Spanish city of Torrevieja. This former fishing village has grown to become one of Spains most popular resorts, with acres of beautiful countryside and miles of spectacular coastlines hugging popular towns, villages and resorts. While Debra is keen on apartments, Gordon would love a townhouse for their budget of £120,000. Laura Hamilton shows them a selection of places that satisfy both of their desires, from a sea-view apartment and a property with a gym and a sauna to a tantalising townhouse

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Holiday host Donna fears that she cannot deliver the luxury stays shes promised in Italy. Theres a housing emergency at Brian and Paulas new French restaurant, and, also in France, Paul and Melissa are put through their paces restoring a once-prestigious hotel

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sue hosts the weeks final visit, at her Victorian townhouse BandB, Brooklands Guest House, in Penrith. The vintage decor is a hit with most of the guests, but things turn bittersweet after a marmalade tasting experience when discussion turns to payment day

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the penultimate night in Manchester, cabaret practitioner Vanessa is turning her modest Mancunian semi into a Chicago speakeasy, with home comfort food planned and a side order of illicit hooch. Richard is blown away by Vanessas outfit and its not long before hes accusing Danny of dressing like a Butlins red coat. Amy and Pauline have a snoop around the house and discover a treasure trove of outfits from Vanessas cabaret shows. The evening really takes off when Vanessas alter ego Coco Deville takes to the floor accompanied by a small band and some ostrich feathers. The piña colada crumble amazes Danny and Richard, and the group learn the subtle art of face yoga. Will Vanessas illicit night of burlesque bravado ruffle feathers?

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Highlights
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan presents action from day six of the Games, held in Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Para alpine skiing giant slalom medals were up for grabs at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, and the mixed team curling event reached its final day of round robin fixtures at Cortina Curling Olympic Stadium

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Natalie Fleet, MP for Bolsover, sets out the governments vision for tackling the cost of living crisis

programme.title: George Clarkes Building Home
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets people who are taking on challenging huge builds for powerful emotional reasons, from being drawn back to an area that theyve always loved or a place with strong family memories to taking on a house thats close to their heart. Young married couple Chris and Ellie have returned to where Chris grew up, in Lancashires Ribble Valley, to build a home together. After months of struggling to find the perfect property, they ended up buying a run-down bungalow from Chriss grandparents - but when it turned out the house wasnt suitable to live in, they began the months-long process of building a new house from scratch

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: As housemaid Emma and young master Edwin fall deeper in love, she struggles to see how they can overcome their class differences. Meanwhile, Adam and Olivia continue their own love affair as Adeles erratic behaviour and paranoia worsen. In the 1970s, 80-year-old Emma reckons with her childrens plan to oust her from the Harte empire - unaware of a secret romance blossoming between her protegee granddaughter Paula and Jim Fairley. Starring Jessica Reynolds and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-three-year-old motorbike enthusiast Scott is rushed in after crashing at high-speed at a track event, and there are concerns he may have suffered spinal injury. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Peter has potentially life-threatening injuries after being crushed under an eight-ton static caravan and 21-year-old hockey player Lizzie has severely broken her ankle during training and needs a reset

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Roofer Simon is in a critical condition after falling off a two-storey building onto concrete. Major trauma consultant Rory and the team try to assess Simons multiple major injuries and work together to try to save his life. Ninety-seven-year-old Doris is in Resus with a worryingly fast and irregular heart rate, while in the Childrens Emergency department, 20-month-old Evelyn has a burn on her finger from a mozzarella stick

programme.title: Millionaire Hoarders
programme.desc: Car fanatic John decides to part with some of his beloved collection to help fund his retirement. Expert and fellow petrol-head Clive spots two classic cars in Johns collection that could raise a huge sum, but will his gamble on an auction sale pay off? In the West Country, Rachel and Ronnie help Caroline, who inherited an inn from her father but has made the difficult decision to part with some of its weird and wonderful contents in order to invest on the building. Ronnie spots a rare animal pelt that could be the solution to Carolines problems - if he can prove its authentic

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eaterys decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brothers trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Molly vs the Machines
programme.desc: Documentary about the death of Molly Russell, the 14-year-old who in 2017 took her own life as a result of depression and negative social media content, and her father Ians fight for online safety. The film focuses on what happened to Molly in the lead-up to her tragic death and the broader economic logic behind AI and big tech as it continues to shape, influence and infiltrate peoples lives

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Isle of Wight, where they meet Helen and husband Dave to restore unwanted items including a metal garden bench and an old water feature

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, married couple Paul and Debbie kick off the competition with a menu of tried and tested dinner party favourites. However, engaged couple Angela and John arent impressed by their simple fare and posh couple Kirsty and James find Pauls behaviour even less palatable. Perfectionists Angela and John cook up dishes inspired by their favourite restaurant meals. Finally, Kirsty and James plan an organic, locally sourced menu. At the table theres a lesson in asparagus eating and the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their guests return their food criticism

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney and judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller present the final, where the three remaining potters create miniature model theatres and take on a tricky throwing technique in their last surprise second challenge. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?
