programme.desc: When his daughter is brutally murdered and legal justice looks unlikely, William Duncan takes the law into his own hands, setting out on a quest for retribution. After killing the street thug who was directly responsible for her death, he finds himself in the middle of a war with the dead mans brother, who is equally hell-bent on getting even. Action thriller, starring Bruce Willis and Thomas Jane

programme.title: Long Gone Heroes (2024)
programme.desc: A battle-hardened special forces operative attempts to rescue his kidnapped niece when a mercenary mission spirals into chaos. Action thriller, starring Eden Brolin, Andy Garcia and Melissa Leo

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop (1984)
programme.desc: Fast-talking Detroit cop Axel Foley heads for Los Angeles after an old friend is murdered before his eyes. His search for the killers leads him into conflict with a seemingly untouchable drug kingpin, but he also has to contend with the local force, who do not want him taking matters into his own hands. Comedy thriller, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, Ronny Cox, John Ashton and Steven Berkoff

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II (1987)
programme.desc: Wisecracking Detroit detective Axel Foley returns to Los Angeles to investigate the shooting of an old friend, and uncovers a connection with a spate of violent robberies carried out by a criminal who leaves a mysterious calling card. Comedy thriller sequel, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, John Ashton, Ronny Cox and Brigitte Nielsen.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop III (1994)
programme.desc: Maverick cop Axel Foley returns to Los Angeles when an investigation into a colleagues murder leads him to a Hollywood theme park. He enlists the help of old ally Rosewood to prove the resort is a cover for a major counterfeiting ring connected to a terrorist conspiracy. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, Theresa Randle and Bronson Pinchot.

programme.title: True Blood
programme.desc: Sookie acts on her attraction to Ben by inviting him to dinner. Meanwhile, Eric counters Burrells brutal anti-vampire initiatives by hitting him where it hurts the most, Andys faerie daughters keep dangerous company, and Bill asks Takahashi to synthesise a new kind of blood

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: Lester hears reports that a man has been dragged into a huge hole in the ground, so the team heads out to investigate and soon comes face to face with a giant burrowing insect causing chaos across the city. Matt devises a plan to draw the creature out so it can be killed, but more than one member of the gang is put in danger as the battle commences. Back at ARC, Philip hires a new lab assistant for Connor, but is there more to her than meets the eye? Sci-fi adventure, starring Ben Miller, Ciaran McMenamin, Andrew-Lee Potts, Hannah Spearritt and Alexander Siddig

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: When a nuclear submarine encounters an anomaly in the sea, the team is called aboard to help, only to come under attack from aquatic reptiles and be transported back to prehistoric times. With external damage to the sub and no electricity, Connor, Matt and Abby face a struggle to repair the craft for the journey home while fending off the marauding creatures. Back at ARC, Lester has to cope with Navy bigwigs threatening drastic action if the crew cannot fix the problem. Sci-fi adventure, starring Andrew-Lee Potts, Hannah Spearritt, Ciaran McMenamin and Ben Miller

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard is torn between his relationship with Bernadette and pleasing his mother - and comes up with a solution that leaves everyone unsatisfied. Leonard rekindles his romance with Rajs sister Priya, while Amy uses Pennys emotions as the basis for a research project

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard decides to tell his mother that he and Bernadette are engaged - but she later collapses, making him fear the shock of the news has caused her to suffer a heart attack. programme.title: Edwardian Farm

American drama, starring Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson and Rex

programme.title: My Life Is Murder

Although the drunk driver involved turned herself in, Alexa believes the accident may be the work of an opportunistic killer. To her surprise, she finds a simple bus timetable holds the clue to whodunit - and why. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team enter the high-stakes world of poker when Bobby Rodrigues, the favourite to win the Caribbean Masters tournament, is poisoned mid-game. As all fellow players are confined to their seats for the entire match, and no other individual is allowed access to the stage during that time, suspicion soon falls upon each player. A novice to poker, Jack enlists Dwaynes assistance in getting the players to show their hand, while Florence, who is forced to keep away from work after an injury, finds her own way to help the case. Nigel Planer, Mark Benton and Zoe Telford guest star

programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regulars Chris Addison, Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Milton Jones, Ed Byrne and Ava Vidal on the topical comedy quiz, with the panellists giving their take on the weeks major news stories

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Warwick Davis, Paul Hollywood, Jason Manford and Joan Bakewell join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Hate Thy Neighbour
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix spends time with two passionate but very different pro-life preachers during a period where the abortion debate has been reignited

programme.title: Hate Thy Neighbour
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix explores the highly-charged issues surrounding the Confederate flag with guidance from an unlikely champion called the Black Rebel

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A new recruit fresh out of training school makes her mark when she uncovers 12kg of cocaine hidden in a car that she has stopped on one of the bridges

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers think they have spotted a trend when they seize drugs from behind the dashboards of two cars. A Mexican woman asks officers for protection

programme.title: Railroad Australia
programme.desc: A traffic jam splits a town in half, causing trouble for veteran driver Bernie Baker. Plus, trainee Allie Hume is working hard to pass her final drivers exams

programme.title: Rick Steins Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary journey by sea and land to explore the Far Easts most diverse food cultures. He begins in Cambodia, where tourists are beginning to return after the fall of the Khmer Rouge regime. As well as discovering the joys of freshly caught swimmer crabs with green peppercorns, he learns how to cook the countrys most famous dish - fish amok - and samples lok lak, made with marinated beef. He also visits the Angkor Wat temple and joins a rice barge to travel down the Mekong River

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers discover the fuel tanks on a truck are full of liquid meth. Three men wearing identical clothing cause suspicion. A man is caught smuggling reptiles

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A woman is suspected of smuggling someone elses baby over the border. Drugs are found hidden in a consignment of carrots. A pill smuggler is caught following an earlier tip-off

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: David Harewood and best friend Fraser James swap the sets of Hollywood for a new challenge in Slovenia where they face some of the most dangerous roads in Europe

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the worlds most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans - some five years before he was announced as the shows new presenter - is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Renaultsport Twingo 133 through a stringent examination in Belfast - with a helping hand from actor Ross Kemp. Richard Hammond samples the delights of racing airport vehicles and James May takes the Vauxhall Insignia VXR for a drive in the company of Margaret Calvert, whose ideas were central to the design of modern British road signs. Film director Guy Ritchie is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and there is also a group test of high-performance off-roaders from BMW, Audi and Range Rover

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes

Melody and Julia disagree about what to do with a teenage mother obsessed with her babys health. Starring Adrian Lewis Morgan

programme.desc: Tom tries to boost his reputation by undertaking a new procedure on an asthmatic patient with severe angina, but Zubin is furious he was kept in the dark about the operation. Rosie is forced to confront her true feelings for Mubbs when she discovers he has an interview at another hospital and could be leaving soon, while love is also in the air for Owen and Lisa after he invites her back to his place for a nightcap

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jordan has to rally the team after introducing an unpopular new policy, while Zoe suspects a boy who fell from a school building had been taking LSD, which head teacher Mrs Haines claims he has been supplying to other pupils. Kirsty suspects a shooting victims return to hospital after falling down the stairs is due to deeper psychological issues, and Ruth plans a night of intimacy with her new husband Edward. Guest starring Josie Lawrence and Michelle Collins

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Quinnan discovers that trying to help a friend might be hindering his own love life

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A live episode of EastEnders in which the Walford residents enjoy Ricky and Biancas wedding reception and the identity of Archies killer is revealed

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: While the Square comes to terms with recent events, an irate Zainab gets stuck in the unit and her waters break. Meanwhile, Ian and Jane discover Lucy alone with Leon

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James is impressed when he visits Granville Bennetts animal hospital with Siegfried, and is interested to learn that it is looking for a new partner. James Grout (Inspector Morse) guest stars, with Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Sarah is caught trying to steal a crucial piece of evidence from Kens office, and Emma tells Jan about Toms true feelings

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Second Stag Night of Doggy Wilkinson
programme.desc: Eric Sykes guest stars as absent-minded Doggy, who is disappointed when his stag night fails to live up to his expectations. However, Clegg, Truly, Alvin and Entwistle come up with a solution and soon rectify the situation. With Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: An innocuous section of woodland soon becomes a legendary site renowned for its ghostly activity, due to the inventive rumours put about by Alvin, Truly and Clegg. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Variations on a Theme of Road Rage
programme.desc: Howard gets a bargain when he buys a used car, but the attention he gets from a burly local is more than he bargained for. Long-running comedy, starring Robert Fyfe and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mattie investigates another murder involving the band when someone is stabbed in the back with a kitchen knife. While searching for clues she finds a recording the lead singer made just before she died and it appears an incident from the past may be the key to solving the case

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: Mo McIntyres grand plan to dig up an ancient stash of paramilitary weaponry and trade the arms for drugs gets under way. However, he is unaware that he is already under surveillance and that MI5 are onto him. Tommy Fosters firearms exam is brought forward before he has time to fully prepare. In a bid to convince Grace to come back to the work, Stevie calls round to her house and finally reveals something intimate about his personal life

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A Home Office pathologist joins the team to help discover the identity of a dead girl found in woodland in 1987. The detectives trace her origins back to the Highlands of Scotland, uncovering evidence of syphilis and a possible connection with local aristocrat Lady Deeley. Meanwhile, Lane confronts his fears by revisiting the crime scene. Detective drama, guest starring Timothy West and Jane Asher

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective and Havers are sent to the Scottish Highlands to investigate the murder of a respected playwright, only to be confronted by glamorous stars from the film industry. As events unfold, they discover sexual jealousy, unstable relationships and numerous lies behind the glossy showbiz facade. Detective drama, guest starring James McAvoy, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl is feeling the effects of two years in prison, prompting Dorien to offer her own brand of advice. Comedy, starring Alun Lewis and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Copper
programme.desc: Corcoran begins to question the motives behind strongholds of the Five Points community, and he solicits coppers of the Sixth Precinct to suss out the truth

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A stranger asks for help with a leaking car radiator shortly before police turn up at the house, but it transpires he has a hidden agenda. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy picks up a lead on a drug dealer operating from a burger van and before long Sun Hills uniform branch are mounting a raid on a skunk factory - where Diane puts herself in great danger by ignoring Callums orders. Back at the station, Phil and Stuart muster as much empathy and sensitivity as they can when CID are called on to sort out a love triangle and find a missing bridegroom in time for his wedding

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel helps a guilt-ridden man troubled by recurring dreams following his involvement in a hit-and-run accident, who feels unable to go to the police for fear of losing his job. Matthew Chambers stars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Archie helps to settle a dispute between a therapist and a neighbour from hell who is causing her to lose business by playing his music too loud. Julia and Nick see an opportunity for the Mill to expand - though not everyone is convinced it is a good idea. Matt Kennard and Diane Keen star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Diane visits an Army hospital to instruct its surgical team on trauma techniques, and immediately takes a shine to a captain, but is also placed in a tricky situation after witnessing a soldier being abused. Back at Holby, Mubbs inadvertently disturbs Owen and Lisas night of passion when he calls on his boss to help with an urgent operation. The surgeon gallantly obliges, but fails to mention the fact he is drunk, risking his career in the process. Guest starring Saeed Jaffrey

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jordan declares the Clinical Decisions Unit open for business, but the new system leaves Adam struggling to keep up - and some poor decision-making only adds to the chaos. Meanwhile, Yuki and Mads form an unlikely partnership and Edward tries to apologise to Ruth by taking her to dinner. Long-running medical drama, starring Michael French, Tristan Gemmill and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy picks up a lead on a drug dealer operating from a burger van and before long Sun Hills uniform branch are mounting a raid on a skunk factory - where Diane puts herself in great danger by ignoring Callums orders. Back at the station, Phil and Stuart muster as much empathy and sensitivity as they can when CID are called on to sort out a love triangle and find a missing bridegroom in time for his wedding

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Masood heroically helps Zainab deliver their son, while Max and Jean worry about Stacey. A heartbroken Dot loses Dotty, Ronnie shuns her family, and Ian teaches Lucy a lesson

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: While Masood shows off his new son, an unexpected visitor arrives. Lucas stops Jordan digging away at Trinas memory, Jane supports Lucy, and Jean and Becca worry about Stacey

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A London banker who is an old school friend of James comes to stay for a long weekend, and gives him advice about going into partnership with Granville Bennett. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: A private investigator spies on Leo and Abby. Meanwhile, Ken schemes against Sir Edward, and Charles questions Sarahs loyalty

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: A carpet magnate is killed in an accident at his warehouse, but his youngest daughter suspects foul play and hires Frank and Luella to investigate her own two sisters

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: In Which Howard Gets Double Booked
programme.desc: Howard discovers he has mistakenly agreed to take Pearl and Marina out to dinner at the same time. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Robert Fyfe, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will the Nearest Alien Please Come In?
programme.desc: Truly, Clegg and Alvin try to convince science-fiction fanatic Kevin that aliens have landed nearby. Starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Mark Curry

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Elegy for Small Creature and Clandestine Trackbike
programme.desc: Howard finds it difficult to hide his new bike from Pearl, and Clegg manages to make things worse. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A missing woman staggers into the police station, claiming a creature is trying to get out. That night her father panics as he sees an alien shape through the window. The next day his body is found in a locked room and Father Brown suspects he has been poisoned. Such events prompt local speculation to mount that Kembleford is under threat of an alien invasion. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Nevison confides in Kevin about the kidnappers ransom demands, making the accountant realise the enormity of the situation he has created. His boss now faces two problems - raising the money and telling his ill wife their daughter has been abducted. Catherines search for Tommy gains momentum when she receives a tip-off regarding his whereabouts, much to the concern of Claire, who thought her sister had put all that in the past, and things are strained when Catherines grandson Ryan makes an unwelcome appearance at a family dinner. Sarah Lancashire stars, with Steve Pemberton, George Costigan, James Norton, Karl Davies, Siobhan Finneran and Joe Armstrong

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A car in which a teacher was murdered five years earlier is returned to its owner - the victims husband. Pullman reopens the unsolved case, hoping to track down the dead womans secret lover, who is known to have had a small tattoo - a clue that eventually leads to a male prostitute. Detective drama, starring Amanda Redman, James Bolam, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: A carpet magnate is killed in an accident at his warehouse, but his youngest daughter suspects foul play and hires Frank and Luella to investigate her own two sisters

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers and Lynley visit Cambridge to investigate the murder of Elena Weaver, a respected lecturers deaf daughter. After a post-mortem reveals she was pregnant, the detectives gradually build a portrait of a promiscuous and playful woman with many admirers - most notably Gareth Randolph, a member of the deaf community who was in love with her, and Victor Troughton to whom Elena was engaged

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A stranger asks for help with a leaking car radiator shortly before police turn up at the house, but it transpires he has a hidden agenda. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Catch Me a Killer
programme.desc: Micki heads to Durban after a series of bodies are found in sugarcane fields. Her increased workload begins to affect her, raising concerns amongst the team

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon foils a mugging at Chigwell station, and ends up befriending the intended victim - who turns out to be financial adviser to Doriens husband

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon gets a passion for democracy - and decides to buy Darryls vote from Tracey for the local by-election. Robert Llewellyn guests stars, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Eric gets involved with an Irish rock band, while Lovejoy takes to the water with Lady Janes friend Victoria Cavero and bravely pops the question - only to receive an unexpected response. Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton and Chris Jury star with guests Joanna Lumley and the members of Hothouse Flowers

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lady Jane persuades Lovejoy to act as driver for an old friend who has to attend her sea-faring brothers burial. The roguish antiques dealer is given the job of selling the late captains Second World War medals, and there is a smart fashion photographer who wants them. However, some drunken sailors then come into the picture and Lovejoy finds himself dealing with the gun-toting Italian Angelo Pantaloni. Light-hearted drama, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: The government faces financial ruin, but that does not seem to worry Sir Humphrey, who goes to extreme lengths to get his latest pay rise scheme approved. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.desc: Jim has his first encounter with the Foreign Office and discovers they are something of a law unto themselves. programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker runs the risk of upsetting the Church of England by recommending one of two rather dubious characters for a new bishop. Meanwhile, Humphrey has his own reasons for wanting a third. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: MI5 reveals its former head was a double agent who worked as a Russian spy. To make matters worse, he had been investigated and given security clearance by Sir Humphrey. Classic satire, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is January 1936, and diplomat Sir Hallam and Lady Agnes Holland (Ed Stoppard and Keeley Hawes) move into 165 Eaton Place - closely followed by his widowed mother and her wayward sister. programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is five months since the Hollands moved into Eaton Place, and Rose is officially installed as housekeeper, while Lady Agnes has manoeuvred the house to the centre of London society - and her joy is compounded by the news she is pregnant. But storm clouds are gathering in Europe, and Lady Persie falls under the spell of Oswald Mosley and his British Union of Fascists, forming an alliance that will drag her into moral and physical peril. Keeley Hawes, Jean Marsh and Claire Foy star

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Martin Clunes stars in the period drama version of The Terminator and Mr Cholmondeley-Warner presents a few handy hints on self defence. Elsewhere, the Scousers plan to rob a building society

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: Eddie has spent the pairs last £11.80 in savings on a second-hand copy of an adult magazine, forcing the duo to endure an evening of boredom and bickering in front of the TV

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: An encounter with a group of children and a pitchfork inspires the boys to make some quick cash by trick-or-treating on Halloween - with the help of an electric cattle prod. Comedy, starring Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Deborahs new student friends make Gary feel long in the tooth and turn Tony green with envy. As ever, the lads latch onto a mature and responsible solution to their problems - this time it is a night out at a rave. Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin star

programme.desc: Billy celebrates his ancestor Robin Hoods birthday by proudly wearing a green suit, and gullible Barry pays tribute in his own way - much to the amusement of everyone else. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Keith Clifford and Mike Grady star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney mourn Grandads death, and feel gratified to see such a good turn-out of friends and relatives at his funeral - even if most people are more interested in the food afterwards. However, one of the guests, a certain long-lost seafaring uncle, ends up outstaying his welcome. Long-running comedy, introducing Buster Merryfield to the cast as Uncle Albert

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Uncle Albert has a nasty fall into the cellar at the Nags Head, but turns his injury into a profit-making opportunity - eagerly egged on by wheeler-dealer Del-Boy. Vintage comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney aspires to be a pop star, taking on the role of the drumless drummer in Mental Mickeys band but, not surprisingly, Del-Boy manages to ruin his chances. With a guest appearance by DJ Mike Read, alongside David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: An encounter with a group of children and a pitchfork inspires the boys to make some quick cash by trick-or-treating on Halloween - with the help of an electric cattle prod. Comedy, starring Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Deborahs new student friends make Gary feel long in the tooth and turn Tony green with envy. As ever, the lads latch onto a mature and responsible solution to their problems - this time it is a night out at a rave. Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy the chalet maid tells Jeffrey she has a burning ambition to become a Yellowcoat, so he arranges an interview for her. Comedy, starring Su Pollard, Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc, Paul Shane and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Martin Clunes stars in the period drama version of The Terminator and Mr Cholmondeley-Warner presents a few handy hints on self defence. Elsewhere, the Scousers plan to rob a building society

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Toby resolves to move out of the Royal Hotel, but the decision places a further strain on his already fraught relationship with Tom. Father-and-son comedy, starring Nigel Havers and Tony Britton

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isnt enough room for her family as well. Llewellyns student helpers disappear, so he hatches a plan to appropriate Boycies staff and the new arrivals in one fell swoop. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch tries to ensure that Godber has not blown his chances of early release after he gets into a fight a week before his parole board hearing. Final episode of the classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: The Green Green Grass

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The gang visits 10 Downing Street to campaign against a Japanese takeover - and Mrs Slocombe gets a chance to demonstrate that Britain has women even more formidable than Mrs Thatcher. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy celebrates his ancestor Robin Hoods birthday by proudly wearing a green suit, and gullible Barry pays tribute in his own way - much to the amusement of everyone else. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Keith Clifford and Mike Grady star

programme.sub-title: Whos That with Barry and Glenda?
programme.desc: Truly jumps to the conclusion that Barry is up to no good after he is spotted acting suspiciously, but his real motive for being in the woods with a suitcase is unfathomable. Long-running comedy, staring Mike Grady, Frank Thornton and Peter Sallis

programme.desc: A roofer falls three metres from a ladder, while a 52-year-old man with end-stage heart failure might not get his life-saving heart transplant

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Johannesburg
programme.desc: Award-winning journalist Louis Theroux visits Johannesburg, where locals have turned to an industry of private security firms after losing trust in the police

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Back from the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: Delimars search for answers leads to an emotional reunion, new revelations and a confrontation with her father

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Today its a 3-course service challenge, featuring their chosen grain from the Murtoa Stick Shed, the impressive heritage grain storage facility theyre cooking in

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a water trough and a cartwheel as they continue their mission to turn unwanted items into hard cash

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dogs neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to help the Beasleys finish work on an incomplete extension to their former council house. The project has taken a back seat to ill health and loss of work over the previous five years, leaving the family on the brink of despair, so local tradespeople, members of the community and designer Charlie Luxton are enlisted to help with the build

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: Honest Renovations
programme.desc: Koey balances a full-time job and a side hustle while raising kids, so when her sister generously gifts her their shared home, she struggles to find time for renovations

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Whitley, who was convicted of killing her brother after an argument. Whitley claims it was self-defence, but Katie learns she left the house and came back with a gun

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part one of two. The contemplative reporter examines life in Miami County Jail, where most of the inmates are awaiting trial. He begins by exploring the large cage-like dwellings on the fifth and sixth floors, which are home to many of the most volatile prisoners. The inmates lives are governed by a gladiatorial code, leading to fights for food and status - a situation the guards say they are powerless to address

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part two of two. The reporter goes deeper into the jail system, meeting an alleged triple murderer facing a possible death sentence. He also follows a group of younger convicts who have avoided prison by pleading guilty and attending a four-month military-style boot camp. Among them is a 14-year-old boy who may face a 10-year sentence for armed robbery if he cannot endure the harsh training and rehabilitation programme

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest. They include a family of 12 in Colorados Rocky Mountains, who have isolated themselves for the past decade

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Documentary series following the paramedics of West Midlands Ambulance Service. Dan and Shane suspect blood poisoning after picking up a seriously ill patient

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Talking to Anorexia
programme.desc: The reporter meets patients at two of Londons largest eating disorder facilities, following them through a daily routine of scheduled eating, weigh-ins and group therapy both on and off the wards. Louis discovers the hold the condition has over them, leaving many unsure if they can recover, or want to

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The pioneering vet treats a 10-year-old beagle unable to stand on its back legs, and when a scan reveals the canine has two diseased discs between three of its neck vertebrae, he operates to fuse the bones. He also fits a bionic knee in an 11-year-old labrador with bone cancer in its leg, and treats a bulldog puppy that has been run over by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old dachshund is rushed to Fitzpatricks by a distraught friend of the owner after being run over by a driver and sustaining life-threatening injuries. A couple arrive with their nine-year-old schnauzer, which is booked in for complex spinal surgery after an earlier operation on its knee, and the owners of a shih tzu bring in their pet, which has been injured in an accident at home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to a village in Conwy, north Wales, to help a widower finish building his home. Since the death of his wife and being diagnosed with cancer, David Williams has been unable to work on the property and lives in a caravan with his young daughter Erica - so the crew helps to make the house habitable with the support of the local community and traders

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In this Children in Need Special, the DIY SOS team joins forces with Radio 2. Their mission is to transform a derelict site into a safe base for a young womens charity in Leeds

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nates day begins in high spirits with a driving assessment supervised by Tony, but events escalate when he fails to keep up with a stolen car, which is later involved in a hit-and-run. The investigations lead to the driver, but also reveal the victims family has been under intolerable strain, and her brother has been making ends meet by dabbling in motor theft and drug-running

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A teenage runaway is found dead from an overdose near a known drug users haunt and fingerprints on the syringe suggest she was murdered. Investigations lead Mickey and Jo to a gang trafficking young girls which is part of a national network, and Jo resolves there will be no more victims. Warren Brown guest stars. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jo bonds with a teenager being groomed by the traffickers and Mickey leads a sting to catch the ringleader of the operation. But when the alarm is raised too early, they have insufficient evidence to hold their man, and news then reaches the police that the gang is moving a large group of girls to Glasgow. The race is on to track them down, but Jos anxiety to save the children clouds her professional judgement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stevie helps Stuart uncover a burglary scam operating from a Santas grotto run by an ex-con, and a few heads are turned when Father Christmas is brought in for questioning. The trail of evidence leads the team via a loan shark and fake passport dealer to a crime kingpin who is planning to skip the country. He is a man Stevie knows from when she worked undercover, and is convinced he is up to something big

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Johannesburg
programme.desc: Award-winning journalist Louis Theroux visits Johannesburg, where locals have turned to an industry of private security firms after losing trust in the police

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Back from the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: Delimars search for answers leads to an emotional reunion, new revelations and a confrontation with her father

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Too Hot to Handle - Part One
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towlers girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. The case progresses well, especially when Stevies ex-boss Jim Rutherford turns up to help. Eventually her cover is blown by a member of the gang as the Sun Hill detectives close in for a sting operation, and Stuart is left questioning his colleagues loyalties when she whisks Towler away on her motorbike and disappears without trace. Continues on Monday

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Dutch DAF 66 - in vivid daffodil yellow - lands at the North Yorkshire showroom

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls on a big day out collecting a 1971 Jaguar E-Type and a 1933 Morgan three-wheeler Supersport, but first theres a flock of 300 chickens to negotiate before he can load up

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agathas footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels though Cumbria, setting sail in a small craft on Lake Windermere with a lifelong devotee of Arthur Ransomes childrens books Swallows and Amazons. At Barrow-in-Furness, Michael investigates the mountains of scrap left behind as iron ore was turned into ships, trains and railway tracks, and discovers researchers are exploring how slagbanks could contribute to reducing atmospheric carbon dioxide.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the last leg of his railway journey around the north-west coast of England, visiting the childhood home of William Wordsworth in Cockermouth, Cumbria, and ending in Maryport, where the Solway Firth marks the border between England and Scotland

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins the hunt for dark matter in the deepest mine in Britain in Boulby, North Yorkshire, then visits Middlesbroughs Transporter Bridge, which offers high views over the Tees estuary and the Cleveland Hills. At Saltholme, Michael joins the RSPB in a hide to observe a visiting bird of prey

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Whitby, where he explores the ports shipbuilding and seafaring heritage, to Robin Hoods Bay, a former smuggling haven

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: In January 1942 the German army lost the battle for Moscow and a furious Hitler sacked or side-lined his leading generals and appointed himself commander-in-chief of the army. He decided on a bold new plan, focusing his forces on seizing the Soviet oil fields and taking the fuel to supply his own troops. Stalin unveiled his own plan to drive the Germans out of the Soviet Union by launching a huge offensive from Leningrad to the Black Sea. Georgy Zhukov warned him that the Red Army was in no shape to attack, but Stalin banished the former chief of the general staff to the front and took control

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salfords MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Eltham Palace, and explores the art deco wing that was built in the 1930s. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of duelling pistols with a story to tell, two rare Fijian war clubs, and a panel of stained glass from the Houses of Parliament which was broken during the Blitz

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the worlds first carbon neutral country by 2021

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Scarborough to explore one of the greatest royal fortresses in England

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FCs star players made history as Britains first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the towns history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Irelands spectacular Causeway coast to begin a new journey, which takes him along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster. He begins in Co Derry on the windswept shores of Loch Foyle, where the largest accumulation of coastal sands in Ireland has formed a system of dunes visible from space. He also learns how scientists are monitoring the site and hears how their work has been picked up by fellow scientists in the United States to help research the surface of Mars

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects five beautiful veteran motorbikes from an enthusiast who is still happily riding in his 90s. A vividly coloured 1955 Nash Metropolitan brightens up the auction room

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1964 Ford Capri Consul coupe comes up for auction. Paul takes a 1957 Leyland Comet truck for a spin, and Dave admits that high on his wish-list is a Citroen 2CV

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The Spitfire, the Hurricane, the Lancaster and Wellington Bombers, the P51 Mustang were all powered by the Rolls-Royce Merlin engine

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Hitler retreats from public life as he faces defeat on the Eastern Front after the Red Armys triumph at Stalingrad, with senior Nazis competing to prove themselves as his most valuable deputy. Meanwhile, student resistance group the White Rose call on the youth of Germany to rise up and overthrow Hitler and the Nazis, and Claus von Stauffenberg is part of a military resistance network who are planning to kill the Fuhrer

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire. Items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems, a Cartier clock, a tiny bowl with a giant price tag, a hat once given to Ronald Reagan and a walking stick belonging to Scottish music hall star Harry Lauder

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from underneath the last Concorde to ever fly at Aerospace in Bristol, which is celebrating 50 years since its maiden flight. Objects brought in to be valued include a Fijian war club, a tiny Chinese vase, a collection of Radiohead memorabilia, a four leaf clover brooch, a rare piece of Venetian glass and a set of silver candlesticks

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks running late as usual, to collect a Triumph TR8 from Kirkby Stephen East Railway Station, with vintage engine ride thrown in for good measure

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire. Items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems, a Cartier clock, a tiny bowl with a giant price tag, a hat once given to Ronald Reagan and a walking stick belonging to Scottish music hall star Harry Lauder

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The adventurer sets off on a journey through the continent, beginning in the remote and little-visited north-east. In Venezuela, Simon meets some of the thousands of migrants fleeing the economic collapse of their country, while he learns that neighbouring Guyana could be on the verge of its own oil boom. He also meets a gun-toting former warlord who is now one of Surinames most successful businessmen, and ends his journey in French Guiana, where he goes to the launch site of the European Space Agency

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: General Von Klinkerhoffen, tiring of the amount of Resistance propaganda in town, decides to restart the local newspaper, and appoints Rene and Alphonse to run it

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle tries to steal the Fallen Madonna from Herr Flick to boost the Resistance funds, while Edith, Yvette and Mimi are photographed for a Spirit of Nouvion competition

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Rene decides to run for the Spanish border but is stopped before he can make it across - and General Von Klinkerhoffen gets hold of the painting. The British airmen become trapped in wine barrels in the cellar of the chateau

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks running late as usual, to collect a Triumph TR8 from Kirkby Stephen East Railway Station, with vintage engine ride thrown in for good measure

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a barely used 1984 Honda Prelude from one of his all-time heroes - motorcycle legend, Ron Haslam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects five beautiful veteran motorbikes from an enthusiast who is still happily riding in his 90s. A vividly coloured 1955 Nash Metropolitan brightens up the auction room

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1964 Ford Capri Consul coupe comes up for auction. Paul takes a 1957 Leyland Comet truck for a spin, and Dave admits that high on his wish-list is a Citroen 2CV

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley debunks the myths behind the American Civil War, revealing how its history has been retold over the years to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division. She reveals how Abraham Lincolns personal views, and the behaviour of his troops towards African Americans, were not as noble as they appeared. She also learns how history was rewritten in a bid to downplay the evils of slavery, and how a 1905 blockbuster film about the Civil War relaunched the Ku Klux Klan with terrifying results

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the glens of Antrim, where he is transfixed by tales of fairies and legends of the sea. At the entrance to Belfast Lough in Whitehead he discovers a museum which tells the story of Irelands railway heritage. He also sees if he has what it takes to join the local rowing club. At the medieval Carrickfergus Castle, Michael learns of the towns important role in the history of Ireland, when William of Orange landed at its harbour and went on to fight the Battle of the Boyne, which is commemorated by Unionists to this day

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Bangor, County Down, en route to the Northern Irish capital of Belfast. Along the way he enjoys live music on a boat, stops off at the imposing parliament building at Stormont and explores the regenerated Titanic Quarter on the Maritime Mile

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey along the Atlantic and Irish Sea coasts of Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. This time, he makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula towards Drogheda and Newgrange, before arriving in capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, where he visits Dublin Castle and Dublin docks

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.sub-title: How to Build a London Cab
programme.desc: With factory access, see how a 21st century electric London Taxi is made in 28 hours

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.sub-title: How to Build the Fastest Car
programme.desc: A team of plucky Brits attempt to prove that theyve built the worlds fastest car

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Dutch DAF 66 - in vivid daffodil yellow - lands at the North Yorkshire showroom

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls on a big day out collecting a 1971 Jaguar E-Type and a 1933 Morgan three-wheeler Supersport, but first theres a flock of 300 chickens to negotiate before he can load up

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agathas footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the glens of Antrim, where he is transfixed by tales of fairies and legends of the sea. At the entrance to Belfast Lough in Whitehead he discovers a museum which tells the story of Irelands railway heritage. He also sees if he has what it takes to join the local rowing club. At the medieval Carrickfergus Castle, Michael learns of the towns important role in the history of Ireland, when William of Orange landed at its harbour and went on to fight the Battle of the Boyne, which is commemorated by Unionists to this day

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Bangor, County Down, en route to the Northern Irish capital of Belfast. Along the way he enjoys live music on a boat, stops off at the imposing parliament building at Stormont and explores the regenerated Titanic Quarter on the Maritime Mile

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey along the Atlantic and Irish Sea coasts of Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. This time, he makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula towards Drogheda and Newgrange, before arriving in capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, where he visits Dublin Castle and Dublin docks

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.sub-title: How to Build a London Cab
programme.desc: With factory access, see how a 21st century electric London Taxi is made in 28 hours

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.sub-title: How to Build the Fastest Car
programme.desc: A team of plucky Brits attempt to prove that theyve built the worlds fastest car

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: An access-all-areas look at how the classic Mini is being manufactured for the electric revolution. What is needed to create the components of a Mini Electric?

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerods strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress footballer Osebi in Manchester, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in Manchester, where they try to win the affections of fashion stylist Courtney by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The opening part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as Tommy Martin talks to him about his journey through football from Sallynoggin to Manchester United to the World Cup, and his relationships with iconic managers Jack Charlton and Alex Ferguson

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery show in which famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the contestants is paired with a chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. In the first episode, Johnny Vegas, Harry Judd, Shirley Ballas and Naughty Boy take part

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Yvonne Cobb and Rupy Aujla at Tyntesfield in Somerset to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day this time. Ainsley collects greengages and plums for his brioche French toast, chargrilled plums and cinnamon creme fraiche, Yvonne adds delicious flavour hacks to a creamy tarragon chicken and French bean salad with roasted rosemary potatoes, and Rupy talks up the health benefits of his grilled mackerel, roasted apple, radish and chilli and pea pesto

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2010

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2011

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. His investigations take him to Spain, where he discovers first-hand that as a consequence of a science experiment gone wrong, the Wels catfish is growing to man-eating proportions and is capable of everything depicted in medieval records

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: As storms close runways, a man has an apparent heart attack on an incoming flight. Can the plane land? Later, a fire drill turns into a real emergency

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Old English sheepdog puppy Jessie has narrowly avoided death but now must undergo emergency surgery. Elsewhere in the hospital, owner Alan is determined to give his 13-year-old crossbreed Jessica as comfortable life as possible as she battles terminal kidney disease. Coco the alsatian is examined to see if she is a candidate to become a blood donor ,while Mary the heifer is operated on to correct a problem with her leg

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Rob, Kate, Bernice and Adam pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the spin-off from The Chase in which contestants take on two, three, four or all five of the quizs resident experts Mark Labbett, Anne Hegerty, Paul Sinha, Jenny Ryan and Shaun Wallace at once. Members of the audience are called out to face five multiple choice questions, with the chance to bank up to £5,000, but if they get the first question wrong they are out. Next, contestants are given the option of playing between two and all five Chasers for increasing amounts of money, but with a decreasing time advantage against them.

programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital has its hands full when a typhoid epidemic breaks out in the community. Things get worse as the diseases source is traced back to the wards and staff struggle to locate it. Dr Weatherill is forced to reprimand Dr Ormerod after catching him sleeping on the job - but also has startling news for him. Drama, starring Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons in Essex try to win the affections of podium dancer Ciara by picking an outfit of their choice for her to wear

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, three singletons go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of self-proclaimed princess Winter. To impress her, they have to dress her, but Winter has no information to go on but the outfits bought. Which of the three competitors will she choose for a prized first date?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The second and concluding part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as he discusses with Tommy Martin the high esteem he was held in for club and country while facing battles on and off the pitch

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones, Catherine Tyldesley, Denise Van Outen and AJ Odudu take to the kitchen in a series of head to head cooking battles. They each attempt to create the perfect Italian dish, and avoid elimination in the Cook Off. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

Ainsley digs up some spuds for his quick flatbread pizza with Charlotte potatoes, rosemary and chilli rings, Daniel brings his French fervour to a pork chop with courgette, tomatoes and cavolo nero, and Ruby celebrates her Indian heritage with her mothers Indian spiced lamb kebab rolls with onion, mint, chilli and coriande

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The fossil record shows that the arapaima fish has roamed the waters of the Amazon River since the Jurassic age, and uses its bony tongue to crush prey against the roof of its mouth before swallowing. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade encountered the species once before and came out second best, so this time he is taking no chances as he investigates every aspect of this huge creature

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: Veteran MI5 operative John Hughes is due to meet a British-Iranian informant who claims to have intelligence about a threat to national security, the man is killed before he can talk. All evidence points towards the death being a gangland dispute, but when John secretly meets with a source, the investigation brings him into dangerously close contact with a formidable Stockport gang. Spy thriller, starring Shaun Evans and Romola Garai

programme.title: The Day Diana Died
programme.desc: Piecing together the critical hours following the news of the Princess of Wales death and examining key decisions made by the Royal Household, newsrooms and Tony Blairs government. This programme looks at the moment the Queen was informed about Dianas death in Paris, and follows the return of her body to British soil. Featuring contributions from Tina Brown, Colin Tebbutt, Dickie Arbiter and Ian Hislop

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms

programme.title: Vaping Nation
programme.desc: One in every 12 people use vapes or e-cigarettes in Ireland, a figure that rises among young people. This documentary investigates the impact of vaping on users health, particularly among teenagers

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to Michelangelos near Newcastle, a 10-bedroom hotel, bar and Italian restaurant set in four acres. Back in 2019, just before the world changed due to Covid-19, Italian brothers Paolo and Giacomo called the Hotel Inspector. The pair had invested 20 years of hard work into the business, battled through Paulos cancer diagnosis and the death of their mother, and still owed almost half a million pounds. Did Alex help to turn their fortunes around?

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game.
